cases.title: Bumgarner v. Presbytery of the Mississippi Valley
cases.synopsis: Bumgarner's BCO 40-5 application regarding proceedings in a judicial case was found administratively out of order and dismissed because BCO 40-3 bars review and control of judicial cases once a complaint or appeal is pending, and because Bumgarner had previously withdrawn his 2012 complaint with an acknowledgment that it could not be resubmitted.

cases.title: Thompson v. South Florida Presbytery
cases.synopsis: Thompson's BCO 40-5 application regarding South Florida Presbytery was found administratively out of order and dismissed because it dealt with proceedings in a judicial case in which a complaint had already been filed, precluding review and control under BCO 40-3.

cases.title: Thompson v. South Florida Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC sustained Thompson's complaint, finding that the Granada Session's judicial commission procedurally erred in imposing excommunication for contumacy before trial because Thompson's behavior did not constitute the immediately censurable kind of contumacy under BCO 32-6.b, and that failing to reach a BCO 38-1 agreement should have led to a trial rather than a pre-trial judgment; the SJC annulled the excommunication and restored the matter to its pre-judgment status as of June 26, 2015.

cases.title: Wills v. Metro Atlanta Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC ruled Wills's complaint against MAP's September 15, 2015 action declaring a prior complaint administratively out of order was itself administratively out of order, because BCO 43-2 requires a complaint to first be made to the court whose act is alleged to be in error, and MAP had not yet had the opportunity to respond; Duncan, Evans, and Donahoe dissented.
